name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Washington, Dc
description_short: NACA is a HUD-approved non-profit housing counselor providing character-based mortgage lending and affordable homeownership programs to underserved communities without credit score requirements.
description_long:
Founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union, the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) has spent 36 years working to close the racial wealth disparity gap through character-based lending and housing advocacy. The organization positions itself as a challenger to predatory lending practices in the mortgage and rental housing markets. NACA operates as both a housing counseling organization and a mortgage lender, helping over 500,000 homeowners achieve affordable homeownership while serving 3 million people overall. They represent 30% of all HUD housing counseling services nationally.

NACA's core mortgage product offers no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no credit score consideration—a stark contrast to traditional lending. As of April 2026, their fixed-rate mortgages are offered at 5.625% for 30-year terms, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year loans. The organization has issued 75,000 NACA mortgages and maintains $20 billion in mortgage commitments. They conduct multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events across the country where prospective members can access counseling services, meet staff, and become NACA Qualified.

What distinguishes NACA is their explicit mission to serve low-to-moderate-income people and people of color who have been historically excluded from homeownership. Rather than focusing on credit scores, they evaluate applicants based on character and circumstances. They also actively campaign against corporate landlords and predatory rental practices, positioning themselves as advocates for economic justice beyond just lending. Their founder and CEO Bruce Marks maintains a public podcast discussing the fight against predatory lenders.

While NACA's no-credit-score mortgage product is genuinely innovative for underserved populations, prospective members should note that accessing their services requires attending multi-day regional events, completing their membership process, and meeting their specific qualification standards. The organization's advocacy mission is genuine, but they operate as both a non-profit counselor and a lender, which differs from traditional HUD-approved counseling agencies.
pros: No credit score consideration for mortgage qualification—character-based lending instead
No down payment, no closing costs/fees, no mortgage insurance required
Below-market fixed rates (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026) with priority membership options
Provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling services nationally with proven track record of 500K+ homeowners helped
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events in multiple states allow in-person counseling and qualification processing
Explicit mission to serve low-to-moderate-income and communities of color historically excluded from homeownership
Member portal for uploading documents, providing information, and checking application status
cons: Requires attending multi-day regional events (Friday-Sunday) to access services and become NACA Qualified—not available online-only
As a non-profit housing counselor operating as a lender, their dual role may create conflicts or complications for some applicants
Website does not clearly explain qualification criteria, timeline, or what 'NACA Qualified' status requires beyond event attendance
Limited geographic accessibility—events are regional and infrequent, not available in all states
No specific information provided about debt-to-income ratios, minimum income thresholds, or other standard lending requirements
best_for: Low-to-moderate-income first-time homebuyers with limited savings or damaged credit histories
People of color and historically underserved communities seeking character-based lending alternatives
Renters facing predatory landlords who want to transition to homeownership through advocacy-focused organization
Borrowers willing to attend multi-day in-person events in exchange for no-down-payment, fixed-rate mortgages
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate-income first-time homebuyers and people of color seeking genuinely affordable mortgages without credit score penalties, who can commit to attending regional multi-day events. The main caveat is that this is not an online-only service—qualification requires in-person participation at scheduled 'Achieve the Dream' events, and their full qualification process and lending requirements are not transparent on the website.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of national volume)
Character-based mortgage lending without credit score requirements
No-down-payment, no-closing-cost mortgages with fixed rates
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' qualifying events in Hartford CT, Little Rock AR, Milwaukee WI, and Fort Myers FL
Member portal for document upload and application status tracking
NACtivist volunteer program and event signup system
Campaign against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors
Podcast content (Breaking Barriers with Bruce Marks, NACA Live American Dream Program) in English and Spanish
$20 billion in mortgage commitments with 75,000 mortgages issued
Community advocacy and economic justice initiatives
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
